skill.name: frontend-aesthetics
skill.description: Guides AI coding tools toward distinctive, production-ready frontend design with stronger typography, color systems, motion, and visual hierarchy.
skill.summary: Improve frontend visual quality with practical design rules and examples.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are documentation examples and keyword matches, not executable behavior. The skill is Markdown guidance with frontend snippets, reference links, and no evidence of malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt-injection text that tries to override security review.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fences Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited locations are Markdown code fences containing CSS, Tailwind, TypeScript, and JSX UI examples, not shell commands or executable scripts run by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is fenced frontend documentation, and no command runner, shell invocation, or script file is present. The static pattern appears to match Markdown backticks rather than executable Ruby or shell syntax.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts are low-risk documentation references. They point to public design-token, component, animation, CSS, and font resources and do not initiate network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in Markdown resource links. I found no evidence that the skill fetches those URLs or sends local data to them.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Only Blocker Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are not confirmed. The cited content is prose, design guidance, Tailwind class names, or summary tables, with no cryptographic implementation, system enumeration, or network reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static labels do not match the semantic context of the cited lines. Review found design terminology and Markdown tables, not security-sensitive algorithms or reconnaissance commands.

content.value_statement: AI-generated interfaces often look generic and lack clear hierarchy. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical rules for typography, color, motion, and depth.
actual_capabilities: Guides font selection away from default generic stacks.
Defines color rules for dark themes, accents, and semantic states.
Provides motion patterns for hover states, page loads, modals, and lists.
Identifies common AI-generated UI anti-patterns with alternatives.
Gives validation checklists for visual hierarchy and consistency.
References design token conventions and frontend resource links.
limitations: Does not execute code or inspect live browser output by itself.
Does not provide a complete brand identity system for every product.
Does not replace accessibility testing, usability testing, or design review.
Some examples assume Tailwind CSS, React, Lucide, or Framer Motion.

use_cases.title: Polish a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Improve dense operational screens with stronger typography, restrained color, clear status states, and subtle motion.

use_cases.title: Refine a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Create a less generic landing page by choosing a distinctive visual direction and avoiding overused AI design patterns.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Generated UI
use_cases.description: Audit generated screens for weak hierarchy, excessive gradients, flat backgrounds, and inconsistent interaction styling.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Simple Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to improve this page visually. Focus on typography, color hierarchy, spacing, and one purposeful motion detail.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Generated Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface with the frontend-aesthetics skill. List the generic AI design patterns you find and propose specific replacements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Design Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to define a frontend direction for this product. Include typography, accent color, surface treatment, motion rules, and component hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Complete Redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced build request for an existing app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the frontend-aesthetics skill to redesign this app screen. Keep the workflow intact while improving visual hierarchy, state feedback, motion, and design consistency.

output_examples.input: A dashboard looks flat and generic.
output: Use a stronger heading font and clearer weight contrast.
Replace gray-heavy dark mode with a true dark base and one accent color.
Add subtle hover feedback, status colors, and staggered list reveals.

output_examples.input: A landing page uses purple-blue gradients and centered cards.
output: Choose one brand accent and reduce decorative gradients.
Create asymmetry with anchored text and varied section rhythm.
Use atmospheric depth only where it supports the product message.

output_examples.input: A team wants standards for generated frontend work.
output: Define approved font pairings, accent colors, motion durations, and card treatments.
Use the checklist to reject unclear hierarchy and excessive rounded shapes.
Document the expected component behavior for repeated screens.

best_practices: Start with the product context before choosing typography, color, and motion.
Use one dominant accent color and reserve semantic colors for status meaning.
Validate the final screen against hierarchy, contrast, motion, and consistency checks.
anti_patterns: Applying purple-blue gradients as the main visual identity by default.
Using the same generic font stack and weight scale across every interface.
Adding decorative motion that does not explain state, focus, or progression.

faq.question: What does this skill help improve?
faq.answer: It helps improve frontend visual quality through typography, color, motion, depth, and anti-pattern guidance.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files are Markdown guidance and frontend examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Is this only for dark interfaces?
faq.answer: No. It emphasizes strong dark-theme examples, but the principles also apply to light themes.

faq.question: Can it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It can guide design decisions, but teams still need product-specific tokens and component standards.

faq.question: Why was the security risk lowered?
faq.answer: The static alerts matched Markdown syntax, prose, and resource links. Review found no confirmed malicious behavior.
